MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS VINH CAO AND MAI CAO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant does not meet their initial burden of showing that the cause of action for fraudulent concealment has no merit because Defendant does not establish that one or more elements of fraudulent concealment cannot be established. The authority cited by Defendant does not establish that Defendant had no duty to disclose the prior flooding events to Plaintiff. Further, Plaintiff shows there is a triable issue of fact on various elements of the cause of action, including whether the Plaintiff was aware of previous flooding events before signing the lease and whether Plaintiff would have acted differently had Plaintiff been aware. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
